title: Permission denied when building from local image using podman remote
body:
### Issue Description

Podman appears to be running under different permissions when called remotely vs locally. When building the first image remotely it works but trying to build a second image FROM the first results in a permissions error when trying to access the first image's layers. Switching off of podman remote causes it to succeed.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Tested on minikube
Steps to reproduce the issue

1. `kubectl apply -f podman-repro-github.yaml`
2. `kubectl exec --stdin --tty podman-repro-test -- bash`
3. `cd builds`
4. Create attached Dockerfiles.
5. podman build -t image-1:latest -f Dockerfile .
6. podman build -t image-2:latest -f Dockerfile.apt .
7. note permissions failure
8. `unset CONTAINER_HOST` to use podman directly without the service
9. podman build -t image-1:latest -f Dockerfile.apt .
10. Note the build now succeeds


Files Used:
podman-repro-github.yaml
```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: podman-repro-test
  namespace: default
spec:
  volumes:
    - name: podman-data
      emptyDir: {}
  containers:
  - name: podman-service
    image: quay.io/podman/stable:v5.1
    command:
      - sh
      - '-ec'
      - >
        printf "[storage]\ndriver = \"overlay\"\ngraphroot =
        \"$GRAPHROOT\"\nrunroot = \"/home/podman/.local/runroot\"" >
        /home/podman/.config/containers/storage.conf && \

        mkdir -p builds/run/podman && \

        unset CONTAINER_HOST && \

        podman system service --log-level=info --time=0 unix:///builds/run/podman/podman.sock
    env:
      - name: CONTAINER_HOST
        value: unix:///builds/run/podman/podman.sock
      - name: GRAPHROOT
        value: /builds/.podman-data
    resources:
      limits:
        cpu: '1'
        memory: 1Gi
      requests:
        cpu: 500m
        memory: 512Mi
    volumeMounts:
      - name: podman-data
        mountPath: /builds
    imagePullPolicy: Always
    securityContext:
      runAsUser: 1000
      runAsGroup: 1000
      capabilities:
        drop:
          - NET_RAW
    stdin: true
  restartPolicy: Never
```

Dockerfile:
```
FROM ubuntu:latest

COPY . .
```

Dockerfile.apt:
```
FROM image-1:latest
RUN apt -y update && apt -y upgrade
```

### Describe the results you received

The second build command has a permissions failure when trying to access files in the image layer

```
ERRO[0000] 1 error occurred:
	* open /builds/.podman-data/overlay/a30a5965a4f7d9d5ff76a46eb8939f58e95be844de1ac4a4b452d5d31158fdea/diff/home/ubuntu: permission denied

 
Error: Post "http://d/v5.1.1/libpod/build?dockerfile=%5B%22Dockerfile.apt%22%5D&forcerm=1&httpproxy=1&identitylabel=1&idmappingoptions=%7B%22HostUIDMapping%22%3Atrue%2C%22HostGIDMapping%22%3Atrue%2C%22UIDMap%22%3A%5B%5D%2C%22GIDMap%22%3A%5B%5D%2C%22AutoUserNs%22%3Afalse%2C%22AutoUserNsOpts%22%3A%7B%22Size%22%3A0%2C%22InitialSize%22%3A0%2C%22PasswdFile%22%3A%22%22%2C%22GroupFile%22%3A%22%22%2C%22AdditionalUIDMappings%22%3Anull%2C%22AdditionalGIDMappings%22%3Anull%7D%7D&isolation=0&jobs=1&layers=1&networkmode=0&nsoptions=%5B%7B%22Name%22%3A%22user%22%2C%22Host%22%3Atrue%2C%22Path%22%3A%22%22%7D%5D&omithistory=0&output=image-1%3Alatest&outputformat=application%2Fvnd.oci.image.manifest.v1%2Bjson&pullpolicy=missing&rm=1&seccomp=%2Fusr%2Fshare%2Fcontainers%2Fseccomp.json&shmsize=67108864&t=image-1%3Alatest&volume=%2Fproc%3A%2Fproc": io: read/write on closed pipe
```

### Describe the results you expected

I expect podman to have permission to access the file it created and be able to build the image.

### Podman in a container

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

on kubernetes where an emptydir volume is used for graphroot

Tested both on Minikube and EKS

### Additional information

This appears to happen based on the storage used for graphroot however I would expect if there was an issue with the backing storage the error would show up when using for remote and local podman. As far as I know emptydir and container storage space on k8s is generally backed by the same drives unless `medium` specifies otherwise

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

podman-remote must copy the entire context dir to the server. Now as you seem to use the home dir of you user this also includes the container storage. However the container storage can contain files with different uid (from /etc/subuid) so your user has not access to them unless they join the podman userns. The remote client never joins the userns as this doesn't make sense.
You likely want to add a .docker/.containerignore file to exclude `.local` to not copy the the container storage to the server.
